source.book: 《9分达人雅思听力真题还原及解析6》

segments.text: Arya: John, how is it going with your research on water hyacinth?
segments.translation: 约翰，你那个关于水葫芦的研究进展怎么样了？

segments.text: John: Well, I went on a field trip on my own and collected the primary data.
segments.translation: 嗯，我自己去做了一次实地考察，收集了一手数据。

segments.text: John: After I came back, I also referred to the published data.
segments.translation: 回来之后，我还查阅了一些已发表的数据。

segments.text: Arya: So what do you know about this plant?
segments.translation: 那你对这种植物都了解到了些什么？

segments.text: John: Water hyacinth grows in tropical countries.
segments.translation: 水葫芦生长在热带国家。

segments.text: John: It has beautiful purple-blue flowers, but everybody hates it.
segments.translation: 它开着漂亮的紫蓝色花，但大家都讨厌它。

segments.text: Arya: Why is that?
segments.translation: 为什么呢？

segments.text: John: Water hyacinth, as the world's worst invasive aquatic plant, has attracted significant attention.
segments.translation: 水葫芦作为世界上最恶劣的入侵性水生植物，引起了广泛关注。

segments.text: John: Millions of these plants grow in rivers and lakes and have brought huge environmental and cultural problems to the local people.
segments.translation: 数以百万计的这种植物长在河流和湖泊里，给当地人带来了巨大的环境和文化问题。

segments.text: John: Farmers cannot travel in boats on the water, and thus cannot fish in it.
segments.translation: 农民没法划船在水上通行，因此也没法在水里捕鱼。

segments.text: John: In addition, heavy free-floating mats of water hyacinth are clogging the dam at the power station in West Africa.
segments.translation: 此外，成片自由漂浮的水葫芦正在堵塞西非发电站的水坝。

segments.text: John: Turbines of the power station are often shut down because of the intertwined leaves.
segments.translation: 发电站的涡轮机常常因为缠绕在一起的叶子而被迫停机。

segments.text: John: Dams often cease to function as a result, severely restricting the electricity generation.
segments.translation: 结果水坝常常无法运转，严重限制了发电。

segments.text: John: Dense mats of water hyacinth also shade the surface and prevent algae growth, thus limiting the production of oxygen and threatening the survival of fish.
segments.translation: 密密麻麻的水葫芦还会遮住水面，阻碍藻类生长，从而限制氧气的产生，威胁到鱼类的生存。

segments.text: Arya: So where did water hyacinth originate from?
segments.translation: 那水葫芦最初是从哪里来的呢？

segments.text: John: Water hyacinth is native to Amazon Basin in Latin America.
segments.translation: 水葫芦原产于拉丁美洲的亚马逊河流域。

segments.text: John: It has spread mainly to the tropics and subtropics throughout much of the world since the 1800s.
segments.translation: 自19世纪以来，它主要扩散到了世界上大部分的热带和亚热带地区。

segments.text: John: Later, it was introduced into Europe and Africa as an ornamental garden pond plant due to its beauty.
segments.translation: 后来，因为它长得漂亮，被作为观赏性的花园池塘植物引入了欧洲和非洲。

segments.text: John: Flowers of this plant were being sold in local markets in African countries as a source of income for women.
segments.translation: 在一些非洲国家，这种植物的花会在当地市场上出售，成为妇女的一种收入来源。

segments.text: John: Since the 1900s, this weed has infested lakes, rivers and creeks of the delta areas in West Africa.
segments.translation: 自20世纪以来，这种杂草侵占了西非三角洲地区的湖泊、河流和小溪。

segments.text: Arya: I guess whoever introduced this plant had never expected the disastrous impact it would have on the local ecological environment.
segments.translation: 我猜当初引进这种植物的人，绝对没料到它会给当地生态环境带来这么灾难性的影响。

segments.text: Arya: What kind of habitat does water hyacinth thrive most in?
segments.translation: 水葫芦在什么样的环境里长得最旺盛？

segments.text: John: Well, it has been discovered that the nutrient-rich environment contributes significantly to the rapid proliferation of this weed, which obtains its nutrients directly from the water.
segments.translation: 嗯，研究发现，富含养分的环境对这种杂草的快速繁殖起了很大作用，它直接从水中获取养分。

segments.text: John: This is normally compounded with a decrease of nutrients along the river banks.
segments.translation: 而这通常还伴随着河岸沿线养分的减少。

segments.text: Arya: Why is that?
segments.translation: 这是为什么呢？

segments.text: John: The main reason for soil nutrient loss is that local residents cut down trees.
segments.translation: 土壤养分流失的主要原因是当地居民砍伐树木。

segments.text: John: Excessive deforestation accelerates soil erosion, which indirectly compounds this issue.
segments.translation: 过度砍伐加速了水土流失，间接地加剧了这个问题。

segments.text: John: Without enough trees to retain nutrients in the soil, frequent rain might partly aggravate this by carrying some of the nutrients into the waterways.
segments.translation: 没有足够的树木来保持土壤中的养分，频繁的降雨可能会把一部分养分冲进水道，从而在一定程度上加重这一状况。

segments.text: Arya: Are there any effective measures to control or even eradicate this weed?
segments.translation: 有没有什么有效的办法来控制、甚至根除这种杂草？

segments.text: John: Yes, a number of approaches have been widely applied throughout the world.
segments.translation: 有，世界各地已经广泛采用了好几种方法。

segments.text: John: Among them, biological control is the most widely favoured long-term control method by introducing a type of insect that feeds on water hyacinth.
segments.translation: 其中，生物防治是最受青睐的长期防治方法，做法是引入一种以水葫芦为食的昆虫。

segments.text: John: So far, Nigeria has used this method for 6 months.
segments.translation: 到目前为止，尼日利亚用这种方法已经六个月了。

segments.text: John: However, water hyacinth won't be removed immediately.
segments.translation: 不过，水葫芦并不会马上被清除掉。

segments.text: John: The side effects of this method on local ecology will remain unforeseen for years because it takes time for this external insect species to reach a density sufficient enough to affect ecological development.
segments.translation: 这种方法对当地生态的副作用要好多年后才会显现出来，因为这种外来昆虫需要时间才能达到足以影响生态发展的密度。

segments.text: Arya: Then what about other approaches?
segments.translation: 那其他方法呢？

segments.text: Arya: Is there a quicker way to control it?
segments.translation: 有没有更快的控制办法？

segments.text: John: Well, the mechanical removal of water hyacinth is seen as the best short-term solution.
segments.translation: 嗯，机械清除水葫芦被认为是最好的短期解决方案。

segments.text: John: This option includes harvesting plants and in-site cutting.
segments.translation: 这种方式包括收割植物和就地切割。

segments.text: John: This method can immediately open physical space for fish and boat traffic.
segments.translation: 这种方法能立刻为鱼类和船只通行腾出物理空间。

segments.text: John: It is, however, inefficient for workers to process extensive areas, and costly to use large cutting and dredging equipment.
segments.translation: 不过，让工人去处理大面积区域效率很低，而且使用大型切割和疏浚设备成本也很高。

segments.text: John: Ironically, it costs even more to dispose of this plant than to remove it.
segments.translation: 讽刺的是，处理掉这些植物比清除它们花的钱还要多。

segments.text: Arya: Can we make use of this weed and turn it into something good?
segments.translation: 我们能不能利用这种杂草，把它变成有用的东西？

segments.text: John: Now scientists think that water hyacinth can be useful.
segments.translation: 现在科学家认为水葫芦是有用处的。

segments.text: John: The plant itself, although more than 95% water, has a fibrous tissue and a high energy and protein content, and can be used for a variety of useful applications.
segments.translation: 这种植物本身虽然含水量超过95%，但有纤维组织，能量和蛋白质含量都很高，可以用于多种有用的用途。

segments.text: Arya: So what do people do with water hyacinth that is initially removed?
segments.translation: 那么，对最初被清除掉的水葫芦，人们会拿它来做什么？

segments.text: John: First, the cut-down water hyacinth is left to dry under the sun for a few days before being mixed with ash and animal manure.
segments.translation: 首先，割下来的水葫芦要放在太阳下晒几天，然后和草木灰、动物粪便混合。

segments.text: John: The mixture can be used as alternative soil rather than fertiliser.
segments.translation: 这种混合物可以用作替代土壤，而不是作为肥料。

segments.text: John: It can grow crops with increased yield.
segments.translation: 它能让作物长得更好、产量更高。

segments.text: Arya: That sounds interesting.
segments.translation: 听起来挺有意思的。

segments.text: John: Mushroom farmers can benefit from water hyacinth as well.
segments.translation: 种蘑菇的农户也能从水葫芦中受益。

segments.text: John: Using the mixture mentioned earlier, mushrooms generally grow much faster so that farmers can harvest mushrooms sooner, which enables them to make money faster.
segments.translation: 用前面提到的那种混合物，蘑菇通常长得快得多，农户就能更早采收蘑菇，也就能更快赚到钱。

segments.text: Arya: I see.
segments.translation: 原来如此。

segments.text: Arya: It can be used to grow oyster and straw mushrooms, right?
segments.translation: 它可以用来种平菇和草菇，对吧？

segments.text: John: Yes.
segments.translation: 是的。

segments.text: John: In India, attempts have been made to grow these types of edible mushrooms organically utilizing water hyacinth.
segments.translation: 在印度，人们已经尝试利用水葫芦有机地种植这几种食用菌。

segments.text: John: These mushrooms provide sufficient nutritional values including minerals and high-quality protein which are essential to people's level of fitness.
segments.translation: 这些蘑菇能提供充足的营养价值，包括矿物质和优质蛋白，这些对人们的健康水平都很重要。

segments.text: Arya: Right.
segments.translation: 对。

segments.text: Arya: Are there any other measures taken to utilise water hyacinth?
segments.translation: 还有没有采取其他措施来利用水葫芦呢？

segments.text: John: In South-east Asia, people feed cows using chopped water hyacinth mixed with other vegetables.
segments.translation: 在东南亚，人们用切碎的水葫芦拌上其他蔬菜来喂牛。

segments.text: John: Then the waste from cows is treated to produce methane gas, which can be used as fuel for cooking, lighting or powering an engine.
segments.translation: 然后牛的排泄物经过处理产生沼气，可以用作烹饪、照明或驱动发动机的燃料。

segments.text: John: And with global warming getting worse, developing alternative sources of energy will be more urgent than ever.
segments.translation: 而且随着全球变暖越来越严重，开发替代能源会比以往任何时候都更加紧迫。

segments.text: Arya: Maybe in the future people will love water hyacinth instead of hating it.
segments.translation: 也许将来人们会喜欢上水葫芦，而不是讨厌它。

segments.text: John: Yes, that's very likely if we can make full use of this weed.
segments.translation: 是的，如果我们能充分利用这种杂草，那很有可能。
